patch_layer_metadata.objective: Close the remaining deterministic-default gaps not covered by layer 1 by formalizing shared-core/thin-wrapper CLI architecture, standard I/O and state defaults, unified packaging policy, mandatory agent-context injection, machine-readable interface contracts, dependency-direction enforcement, and a compatibility/audit stack.

items: Added shared_core_architecture_contract top-level section defining shared-core, thin-wrapper, standard I/O, concurrent-state, and packaging defaults.
Added agent_context_contract top-level section defining required CLI agent context files, interface contracts, and post-write validation hooks.
Added compatibility_enforcement_stack top-level section defining static-analysis gates, boundary-schema policy, dependency-direction rules, audit loop, and CI/pre-commit enforcement.
Extended step contracts with context_binding, boundary_contract_binding, packaging_binding, and state_coordination_binding.
Added GATE-CFGCTX-001 through GATE-CFGCTX-005 for deterministic context and compatibility enforcement.
patches.purpose: Record layer-2 breaking changes.

cli_wrapper_rules: CLI entrypoints may parse args/options and map them to shared-core function calls only.
Business logic in CLI wrapper is forbidden except input normalization and result presentation.
Side effects at import time are forbidden.

patches.purpose: Make shared-core/thin-wrapper, I/O, state, and packaging defaults first-class.
Force deterministic codebase context injection for CLI agents.
Add structural codebase-compatibility controls.
Block execution when deterministic context or compatibility stack is missing.
Require pattern mappings to bind into the new deterministic context layer.
Make context, contracts, and packaging auditable.
Detect missing deterministic-context controls and compatibility violations.

items.purpose: Shared core / thin wrapper architecture is declared and valid
Agent context files and contract artifacts are declared and valid
Boundary schema policy and dependency direction rules are valid
Packaging policy and entry-point declarations are valid
Audit/compatibility stack passes and reports no drift

patches.purpose: Add mandatory layer-2 validation gates.

python_packages_append.name: pre-commit
python_packages_append.purpose: local hook enforcement for deterministic validation stack

patches.purpose: Pin additional tooling required by the compatibility stack.
Require step-level declaration of context, boundaries, packaging, and state coordination.

items: Shared-core / thin-wrapper architecture contract for CLI-oriented implementations
Mandatory agent-context injection contract for CLI coding agents
Compatibility enforcement stack covering static analysis, dependency layering, boundary schemas, and drift audits
patches.purpose: Reflect layer-2 capabilities.

items: DETERMINISTIC CONTEXT INJECTION: Agents must receive canonical codebase context through validated context artifacts before execution or code generation.
SHARED-CORE CLI ARCHITECTURE: CLI entrypoints are wrappers; business logic lives in shared-core modules with explicit boundary contracts.
patches.purpose: Add the missing deterministic-default principles.
Make the three layer-2 sections required.

items.purpose: Defines shared-core / thin-wrapper architecture, standard I/O handoff rules, concurrent-state defaults, and packaging requirements for CLI-oriented implementations.
Defines required context files, contract artifacts, and post-write validation hooks that must be injected into every CLI-agent session.
Defines the enforced static-analysis, boundary-schema, dependency-direction, audit, and CI/pre-commit controls for codebase compatibility.

patches.purpose: Document new required sections.

new_value.description: Shared-core / thin-wrapper deterministic architecture contract for CLI-oriented implementations.

structure.shared_core_required: boolean - business logic must reside outside CLI wrappers
structure.thin_cli_wrapper_required: boolean - CLI wrappers may only normalize input, invoke shared-core, and present output
structure.standard_io_contract: object - allowed handoff modes and machine-output defaults
structure.concurrent_state_policy: object - required backend and concurrent-writer policy

new_value.purpose: Prevent business-logic drift into entrypoints and standardize handoff/state/packaging behavior.

patches.purpose: Define shared-core architecture semantics.

new_value.description: Deterministic context-injection contract for CLI coding agents.

structure.required_context_files: array - files such as CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .aider.conf
structure.minimum_required_context_sections: array - mandatory rule categories inside the context files
structure.required_contract_artifacts: array - machine-readable interface and schema stubs
structure.post_write_hook_command: string - command every agent must run before marking work complete

new_value.purpose: Ensure agents receive the same codebase constitution every session.

patches.purpose: Define context-injection semantics.

new_value.description: Compatibility stack enforcing static analysis, boundary schemas, dependency layering, and audit loops.

structure.boundary_schema_policy: object - shared boundary types and schema directory
structure.dependency_direction_policy: object - layer order and forbidden import classes
structure.drift_audit_loop: object - audit script, report path, CI/pre-commit requirements

new_value.purpose: Make codebase compatibility structural rather than advisory.

patches.purpose: Define compatibility-stack semantics.

context_binding.description: Binding to the context profile required for this step

boundary_contract_binding.description: References to interface contracts, schemas, and dependency-rule profiles for this step

packaging_binding.description: Packaging/entry-point declaration for executable tools produced or modified by this step

state_coordination_binding.description: State backend and concurrent-write policy required by this step

patches.purpose: Make layer-2 fields part of the formal step contract.

items.name: Shared Core Architecture
items.purpose: Verify shared-core/thin-wrapper, standard-I/O, concurrent-state, and packaging defaults are declared and valid

items.name: Agent Context Contract
items.purpose: Verify required context files and machine-readable contract artifacts are declared

items.purpose: Verify boundary-type policy and dependency-direction rules

patches.purpose: Add layer-2 gates to gate registry.

items.action: Resolve deterministic context and shared-core architecture bindings
tasks: Bind context profiles and required context files
Bind boundary contracts and dependency-rule profile

patches.purpose: Integrate layer-2 resolution into plan generation workflow.

instruction_delta: State that v3.3 additionally requires deterministic shared-core / thin-wrapper architecture rules, mandatory agent-context injection, and a compatibility enforcement stack.
patches.purpose: Extend overview with layer-2 scope.

instruction_delta: Add a principle that CLI-oriented implementations must separate shared-core business logic from thin entrypoint wrappers.
Add a principle that every agent session must load validated context artifacts before code generation or modification.
Add a principle that boundary types, dependency direction, and audit loops are mandatory structural controls, not optional quality checks.
patches.purpose: Promote layer-2 controls to general principles.

markdown_body_lines: Populate this section whenever the plan involves CLI tools, wrappers, commands, or executable entrypoints.
Set `shared_core_required: true` unless the step is explicitly `NOT_AUTOMATABLE`.
Set `thin_cli_wrapper_required: true` and ensure wrappers only parse input, invoke shared-core code, and present output.
Use SQLite as the default shared-state backend for simultaneous local writers unless a stronger managed store is explicitly justified.
patches.purpose: Tell the AI how to fill the new architecture contract.

markdown_body_lines: Populate `required_context_files` with `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `.aider.conf` when CLI coding agents are part of execution or remediation.
Treat these files as mandatory governance inputs, not convenience documentation.
Also declare required machine-readable contract artifacts such as Python stubs, JSON schemas, TypedDict definitions, or Pydantic models.
patches.purpose: Teach the AI to formalize context injection.

markdown_body_lines: Populate required static-analysis commands, boundary-schema rules, dependency-direction policy, and drift-audit configuration.
Do not mark a tool-producing or code-modifying step complete unless the declared post-write hook command passes.
Treat import-layering and boundary-schema violations as structural failures, not warnings.
patches.purpose: Teach the AI to fill the compatibility stack.

instruction_delta: Require `context_binding`, `boundary_contract_binding`, `packaging_binding`, and `state_coordination_binding` for every executable step that generates or modifies code, CLI tools, contracts, or schemas.
Reject steps that create CLI entrypoints without shared-core binding or packaging binding.
Reject steps that share flat-file mutable state across concurrent writers.
patches.purpose: Make layer-2 step fields mandatory.

instruction_delta: Add GATE-CFGCTX-001 through GATE-CFGCTX-005 to plans that involve CLI coding agents, code generation, or code modification.
These gates must be listed in `pipeline_boundary_contract.validation_required_before_execution`.
patches.purpose: Require layer-2 gates.

instruction_delta: Remind the AI that context files, contract stubs, boundary schemas, and dependency rules are mandatory governance inputs.
Remind the AI that packaging and entry-point declarations are required for executable CLI tools.
Remind the AI that compatibility audits must feed back into subsequent repair steps without changing approved behavior contracts.
patches.purpose: Reflect layer-2 rules in final AI reminders.

context_files.purpose: Canonical context template containing module contracts, naming conventions, forbidden patterns, import rules, gate commands, and definition of done.
Alternate agent-context template for tools that consume AGENTS.md.
Canonical Aider context/profile template aligned with the same governance fields.

contracts.purpose: Machine-readable example boundary contract for inter-module I/O.
Stub pattern for module interface contracts that agents must consume.

configs.purpose: Dependency-direction contract enforcing cli -> services -> core -> schemas layering.
Defines console-script entry points and package metadata for unified packaging.
Local hook runner for deterministic validation stack.

schemas.purpose: Validate shared-core/thin-wrapper, I/O, state, and packaging contract.
Validate required context files and contract artifacts.
Validate static-analysis, layering, audit, and CI/pre-commit controls.
Validate step-level boundary contract binding object.

scripts.purpose: Validate shared-core/thin-wrapper contract, handoff modes, state backend, and packaging profile.
Validate context files, required sections, and contract-artifact declarations.
Validate boundary-schema declarations and dependency-direction policy.
Validate compatibility_enforcement_stack section and required command declarations.
Run deterministic drift audit against contracts, imports, schemas, and registry references.

tests.purpose: Reject business logic in wrappers, missing SQLite default, or absent packaging declarations.
Reject missing context files, missing sections, or absent contract artifacts.
Reject forbidden import directions or missing boundary schema declarations.
Reject missing entry points or loose unregistered scripts.
Verify drift audit detects declared violation classes and emits deterministic reports.

template_acceptance: shared_core_architecture_contract, agent_context_contract, and compatibility_enforcement_stack exist at top level and validate against schema.
Every executable code-producing or code-modifying step can declare context_binding, boundary_contract_binding, packaging_binding, and state_coordination_binding.
pipeline_boundary_contract blocks execution when deterministic context or compatibility controls are missing.
technical_spec_acceptance: Root structure marks the three new layer-2 sections as required.
Section purpose map documents all three new sections.
Step contract definition includes the four new layer-2 binding objects.
Gate registry documents GATE-CFGCTX-001 through GATE-CFGCTX-005.
instruction_acceptance: Instructions explicitly require shared-core/thin-wrapper separation for CLI-oriented implementations.
Instructions explicitly require context files and machine-readable contract artifacts for CLI coding agents.
Instructions require compatibility stack and audit loop population when code generation or modification is planned.
runtime_acceptance: validate_shared_core_architecture.py exits non-zero on missing shared-core/thin-wrapper declarations.
validate_agent_context_contract.py exits non-zero on missing context files or contract artifacts.
audit_contracts.py emits deterministic evidence under .state/evidence/context/ and fails on forbidden import directions or missing boundary schemas.

failure_conditions: Any of the three new top-level layer-2 sections are missing after patch application.
Any executable CLI-producing or code-modifying step lacks the required layer-2 binding objects when applicable.
Layer-2 gates are absent from validation_gates or omitted from pipeline boundary validation requirements.
Context files remain advisory rather than execution-blocking governance inputs.
Shared mutable flat files remain allowed for concurrent writers without explicit justified exception.
Packaging policy allows loose unregistered executable scripts without pyproject entry-point governance.
